title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, Mavericks rookie, expressed his love for Dallas, dismissing the idea of joining the Celtics.
Flagg grew up in Maine, supporting the Celtics, but enjoys visiting Boston as an opposing player.
Flagg averages 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds; he's under contract with the Mavericks until at least 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a 42-week absence, scoring 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's approach, despite shooting 6-for-16 with six missed early shots.
The Celtics beat the Mavericks with a 25-point lead, moving closer to postseason with Tatum back in action.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup 10 months post-Achilles injury, starting in the 120-100 victory over the Mavericks.
Tatum contributed 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists, showing limited rust but boosting team performance significantly.
Nikola Vucevic fractured his finger, requiring surgery; Jaylen Brown led scoring with 24 points, aiding Celtics' strong performance.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney didn't make major trades at the deadline, focusing on long-term rebuilding strategies.
Boston traded Brett Harrison, Jackson Edward, and a draft pick for Lukas Riechel, Alexis Gendron, Massimo Rizzo.
The Bruins have four first-round picks over the next two years, positioning them for future success.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby is reportedly being traded from the Raiders to the Ravens for two first-round picks.
Crosby, a five-time Pro Bowler, expressed interest in joining the Patriots or Cowboys, but Baltimore made a better offer.
The Patriots, who have $60 million in cap space, are expected to pursue other trades or signings in free agency.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic broke his right ring finger and will be out for about a month.
Vucevic sustained the injury during a post-up against Moussa Cisse in the game against the Mavericks.
Jayson Tatum's return coincides with Vucevic's absence, affecting the Celtics' front-court depth.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup after a 297-day recovery from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum played six minutes in the first quarter against the Dallas Mavericks, recording two assists and two rebounds.
The Celtics, second in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record, welcomed Tatum back to bolster their playoff campaign.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, per ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade addresses the Bears' need for a new center after Drew Dalman's surprise retirement.
Patriots may replace Bradbury with Jared Wilson, potentially freeing up $5.7 million in cap space.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix loses 'Arrested Development' streaming rights on March 15, available for viewing until then.
'Hamnet' explores a fictional account of Shakespeare's family tragedy, now streaming on Peacock.
Hulu streams 'It Was Just An Accident,' a political critique by Iranian director Jafar Panahi.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics starting lineup for his regular-season debut against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication during his recovery, without mentioning any minutes restriction.
The Celtics celebrated Tatum's return while focusing on their game against the Mavericks, featuring Maine native Cooper Flagg.
